name: Styles
summary: The Styles class contains methods for manipulating a collection of styles in a model. Typically, you will access this via the active_model: There are two objects of this class that play important roles: the #selected_style and the #active_style. The latter is a temporary copy made from the #selected_style that allows the user to edit the style without committing to save anything. To save the changes, one should use #update_selected_style.

methods.summary: The #[] method is used to retrieves a style by either name or index.
methods.remarks: The #[] method is used to retrieves a style by either name or index.

params.name: name
params.doc: The name of the style to retrieve.

methods.name: active_style
methods.summary: The #active_style method is used to retrieve the active style.
methods.remarks: The #active_style method is used to retrieve the active style. While #selected_style is the style being selected in the Style Browser, the ##active_style is a different object also including any unsaved style changes. These changes are silently dropped once a new style is selected. To save these changes to the selected style, call ##update_selected_style.

methods.name: active_style_changed
methods.summary: The #active_style_changed method tells you if the active style has been edited by the user since it was last saved.
methods.remarks: The #active_style_changed method tells you if the active style has been edited by the user since it was last saved.

methods.name: add_style
methods.signature: add_style(filename, select) => Boolean | add_style(filename, select = false) => Sketchup::Style?
methods.summary: The #add_style method is used to create and load a style from the given file.
methods.remarks: The #add_style method is used to create and load a style from the given file.

params.name: filename
params.doc: The file path to the style file.

params.name: select
params.doc: true if you want to set the style to be the active style.

methods.name: count
methods.summary: Note: Since SketchUp 2014 the count method is inherited from Ruby's Enumerable mix-in module.
methods.remarks: Note: Since SketchUp 2014 the count method is inherited from Ruby's Enumerable mix-in module. Prior to that the #count method is an alias for #length.

methods.name: each
methods.summary: The #each method is used to iterate through styles.
methods.remarks: The #each method is used to iterate through styles.

methods.name: length
methods.summary: The #length method is an alias of #size.
methods.remarks: The #length method is an alias of #size.

methods.name: parent
methods.summary: The #parent method is used to return the model for the styles.
methods.remarks: The #parent method is used to return the model for the styles.

methods.name: purge_unused
methods.summary: The #purge_unused method is used to remove unused styles from the model.
methods.remarks: The #purge_unused method is used to remove unused styles from the model.

methods.name: remove_style
methods.summary: The #remove_style method is used to remove a Sketchup::Style from the Sketchup::Styles.
methods.remarks: The #remove_style method is used to remove a Sketchup::Style from the Sketchup::Styles.

throws.when: If the style is not found in the Styles collection.
If the styles contains only one style.

methods.name: size
methods.summary: The #size method is used to retrieve the number of styles in the collection.
methods.remarks: The #size method is used to retrieve the number of styles in the collection.

methods.name: update_selected_style
methods.summary: The #update_selected_style method commits the current style settings to the style selected in the Style Browser.
methods.remarks: The #update_selected_style method commits the current style settings to the style selected in the Style Browser.

properties.name: selected_style
properties.summary: The #selected_style method is used to retrieve the style currently selected in the Styles Browser.
properties.remarks: The #selected_style method is used to retrieve the style currently selected in the Styles Browser.
